mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3% monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting central bank intervention and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: A 1.5% quarterly decline in real GDP (GDPC1) signals economic contraction, triggering fiscal stimulus expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 50%+ of instances where GDPC1 declines > 1.5% quarterly.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS above historical thresholds signal inflationary pressures requiring aggressive rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 240 and FEDFUNDS > 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust manufacturing activity, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO rises > 2% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% monthly increase in residential construction (HOUST) signals strong housing market demand, boosting consumer wealth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 50%+ of instances where HOUST rises > 10% monthly during expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A 150k monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals strong labor market dynamics, driving consumer spending and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 50%+ of instances where PAYEMS rises > 150k monthly.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% monthly decline in real sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer demand, prompting monetary policy accommodation and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 50%+ of instances where RSAFS declines > 5% monthly.
